revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[he:Ч“Ч•Ч§ЧҳЧ•ЧЁ ЧңЧ—ЧһЧ© Ч”Ч§ЧңЧҗЧЎЧҷЧ§Ч•ЧӘ]][[ja:дә”зөҢеҚҡеЈ«]][[ko:мҳӨкІҪл°•мӮ¬]][[zh:дә”з»ҸеҚҡеЈ«]]


{{Confucianism|qq=}}
'''Doctor of the Five Classics''' (дә”зөҢеҚҡеЈ«) is one of the official positions of ancient {{ill|China|ja|дёӯеӣҪ|1=China (region)|2=ja|3=дёӯеӣҪ|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|qid=Q29520}}. During the {{ill|Former Han|ja|еүҚжјў|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Han|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Western Han|qid=Q1072949}} period, it was established as a subordinate post under the {{ill|Ministry of Ceremonies (China)|en|Ministry of Ceremonies (China)|qid=Q10941242|lt=Minister of Ceremonies|1=Ministry of Ceremonies (China)}}. It was a scholarly office responsible for teaching the Confucian canon, the {{ill|Five Classics|ja|дә”зөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Classics|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Five Classics|qid=Q1148214}} (Classic of Poetry, Book of Documents, Book of Rites, I Ching, and Spring and Autumn Annals).
== Overview ==
=== China ===
The Doctors were officials originating from the Qin period, knowledgeable in both ancient and modern matters, and their opinions were frequently sought. In the early Han, it is believed that not only the Confucian canon but also the canons of other schoolsвҖ”such as the {{ill|Hundred Schools of Thought|en|Hundred Schools of Thought|qid=Q864947|lt=Hundred Schools of Thought|1=Hundred Schools of Thought}}вҖ”were established for scholarly officials. During the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Wen of Han|en|Emperor Wen of Han|qid=Q7221|lt=Wen Emperor|1=Emperor Wen of Han}} and {{ill|Emperor Jing of Han|en|Emperor Jing of Han|qid=Q7224|lt=Jing Emperor|1=Emperor Jing of Han}}, the texts {{ill|Classic of Poetry|en|Classic of Poetry|qid=Q465108|lt=Classic of Poetry|1=Classic of Poetry}}, {{ill|I Ching|en|I Ching|qid=Q181937|lt=I Ching|1=I Ching}}, and {{ill|Spring and Autumn Annals|en|Spring and Autumn Annals|qid=Q747720|lt=Spring and Autumn Annals|1=Spring and Autumn Annals}} had already been adopted for official study.
In the 5th year of {{ill|Kangyuan|ja|е»әе…ғ (жјў)|lt=Kangyuan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kangyuan|1=Jianyuan (era)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q911988}}, ({{ill|136 BC|ja|зҙҖе…ғеүҚ136|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/136 BC|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), {{ill|Emperor Wu of Han|en|Emperor Wu of Han|qid=Q7225|lt=Wudi|1=Emperor Wu of Han}}, having accepted the proposal ('''Tianren Sansu''') of {{ill|Dong Zhongshu|en|Dong Zhongshu|qid=Q445626|lt=Dong Zhongshu|1=Dong Zhongshu}}, established the Doctor of the Five Classics.
Within Confucianism, it was traditionally believed that this move supplanted the other schools of thought in favor of Confucianism alone (the so-called {{ill|State Endorsement of Confucianism|ja|е„’ж•ҷгҒ®еӣҪж•ҷеҢ–|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/State Endorsement of Confucianism|qq=}}); however, recent studies suggest that it was merely a matter of increasing the number of Doctors of the Five Classics within the scholarly establishment. Initially, there were only five, and during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Xuan of Han|en|Emperor Xuan of Han|qid=Q7230|lt=Xuan Emperor|1=Emperor Xuan of Han}}, the number was increased to twelve. Under the Doctors of the Five Classics, apprentice scholars (еҚҡеЈ«ејҹеӯҗе“Ў) were appointed. In the Later Han, fourteen Doctors were established ({{ill|Han_dynasty#Eastern_Han|en|Han_dynasty#Eastern_Han|qid=Q7209|lt=Later Han|1=Han dynasty}}{{citation needed|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Later Han|qq=}}).
{| class="wikitable"
|+Fourteen Doctors of the Later Han {{sfn|Fukui|2005|p=223|qq=}}

!Book of Documents

!Book of Rites
!Spring and Autumn Annals

| пјҲ{{ill|Zhang Shu|ja|ејөеҸ”|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhang Shu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}пјү
|-

| {{ill|Cai Yi|ja|и”Ўзҫ©|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cai Yi|1=Cai Yi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11621830}}
| rowspan="3" |

| {{ill|Wei Xian|ja|йҹӢиіў|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wei Xian|1=Wei Xian|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11663951}}
| rowspan="2" | 
|-
| {{ill|Ouyang Gao|ja|ж¬§йҷҪй«ҳ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ouyang Gao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
| {{ill|Jiang Weng|ja|жұҹзҝҒ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiang Weng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

|-
| {{ill|Shi Chou|ja|ж–Ҫи®Һ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Chou|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
| {{ill|Ouyang Diyu|ja|ж¬§йҷҪең°дҪҷ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ouyang Diyu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
| {{ill|Wang Shi|ja|зҺӢејҸ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wang Shi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
| rowspan="6" | 
| {{ill|Yan Pengzu|ja|еҺіеҪӯзҘ–|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yan Pengzu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
|-
| rowspan="5" | 
| {{ill|Lin Zun|ja|жһ—е°Ҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lin Zun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
| {{ill|Zhang Chang'an|ja|ејөй•·е®ү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhang Chang'an|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
| {{ill|Zhou Qing|ja|е‘Ёж…¶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhou Qing|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
|-
| {{ill|Kong Ba|ja|еӯ”иҰҮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kong Ba|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
| {{ill|Xue Guangde|ja|и–ӣеәғеҫі|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xue Guangde|1=Xue Guangde|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11622242}}
| {{ill|Ding Xing|ja|дёҒе§“|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding Xing|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
|-
| {{ill|Zhang Shanfu|ja|ејөеұұжӢҠ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhang Shanfu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
| {{ill|Shizigong|ja|йЈҹеӯҗе…¬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizigong|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
| rowspan="3" | 
|-
| rowspan="2" | 
| {{ill|Wang Ji|ja|зҺӢеҗү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wang Ji|1=Wang Ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11572933}}
|-
| {{ill|Hou Cang|ja|еҗҺи’ј|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hou Cang|1=Hou Cang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21060890}}
|}
=== Baekje ===
According to the гҖҺ{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}гҖҸ, in {{ill|513|en|513|qid=Q32887|lt=513|1=513}}, [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] presented to the {{ill|Wakoku|en|Wakoku|qid=Q10888424|lt=Wa Kingdom|1=Wakoku}} the Doctor of the Five Classics {{ill|Duan Yang'er|ja|ж®өжҘҠзҲҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Duan Yang'er|1=Duan Yang'er|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q19939662}}; three years later, {{ill|Duan Yang'er|ja|ж®өжҘҠзҲҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Duan Yang'er|1=Duan Yang'er|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q19939662}} was recalled, and in his stead {{ill|Han Gao'anmo|ja|жјўй«ҳе®үиҢӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han Gao'anmo|1=Han Gao'anmo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q12584316}} was sent. In {{ill|554|en|554|qid=Q32167|lt=554|1=554}}, he was replaced by {{ill|Ma Ding'an|ja|йҰ¬дёҒе®ү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ma Ding'an|1=Ma Ding'an|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119380477}}, and {{ill|Wang Daoliang|ja|зҺӢйҒ“иүҜ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wang Daoliang|1=Wang Daoliang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119380470}}, {{ill|Wang Liugui|ja|зҺӢжҹіиІҙ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wang Liugui|1=Wang Liugui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119380471}}, {{ill|Wang Baosun|ja|зҺӢдҝқеӯ«|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wang Baosun|1=Wang Baosun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119380473}}, {{ill|Wang Youluota|ja|зҺӢжңүгҘ„йҷҖ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wang Youluota|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зҺӢжңүгғӘгғ§гӮҰйҷҖ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зҺӢжңүгғӘгғ§гӮҰйҷҖ|1=Wang Youluota|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119380475}}, {{ill|Pan Liangfeng|ja|жҪҳйҮҸиұҠ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pan Liangfeng|1=Pan Liangfeng|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119380518}}, and {{ill|Ding Youtuo|ja|дёҒжңүйҷҖ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding Youtuo|1=Ding Youtuo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119380519}} were presented to the {{ill|Wakoku|en|Wakoku|qid=Q10888424|lt=Wa Kingdom|1=Wakoku}} (presented here means вҖңto offer tributesвҖқ).<ref>{{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|author=жңҚйғЁж•ҸиүҜ|authorlink=жңҚйғЁж•ҸиүҜ|date=1981-10-01|title=ж—Ҙжң¬еҢ»еӯҰеҸІз ”з©¶дҪҷи©ұ|publisher=гҖҗз§‘еӯҰжӣёйҷўгҖ‘|ISBN=4760301003|page=22|url=https://books.google.co.jp/books?id=nIYL5t84rJ4C&pg=PA22=onepage&q&f=false#v=onepage&q&f=false|qq=}}</ref><ref name="дә”зөҢеҚҡеЈ«"/>
It has been suggested that the presentation of the Doctors of the Five Classics was a reciprocal gesture in response to the {{ill|Wakoku|en|Wakoku|qid=Q10888424|lt=Wa Kingdom|1=Wakoku}}вҖҷs cession of {{ill|Mimana|en|Mimana|qid=Q1790223|lt=Imna|1=Mimana}} to [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] between {{ill|512|en|512|qid=Q31993|lt=512|1=512}} and {{ill|513|en|513|qid=Q32887|lt=513|1=513}}.<ref name="дә”зөҢеҚҡеЈ«">{{Kotobank|дә”зөҢеҚҡеЈ«|qq=}}</ref>
On the other hand, textual critics who scrutinize the historical narrative presented in the гҖҺ{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}гҖҸ argue that the legend and tradition claiming that the Doctors of the Five Classics were dispatched from Baekje during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|qid=Q349277|lt=Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}} and {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|qid=Q329792|lt=Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}} are difficult to accept as factual.<ref>{{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|editor1=з”°дёӯеҒҘеӨ«|editor1-link=з”°дёӯеҒҘеӨ«|editor2=зҹідә•жӯЈж•Ҹ|editor2-link=зҹідә•жӯЈж•Ҹ|date=2009-01-01|title=еҜҫеӨ–й–ўдҝӮеҸІиҫһе…ё|publisher=гҖҗеҗүе·қејҳж–ҮйӨЁгҖ‘|ISBN=978-4642014496|page=356|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|author=ж–Һи—ӨжӯЈдәҢ|authorlink=ж–Һи—ӨжӯЈдәҢ|date=2006-07-01|title=ж—Ҙжң¬зҡ„иҮӘз„¶иҰігҒ®з ”з©¶ еӨүе®№гҒЁзөӮз„ү|series=гҖҗж–Һи—ӨжӯЈдәҢи‘—дҪңйҒёйӣҶгҖ‘4|publisher=гҖҗе…«еқӮжӣёжҲҝгҖ‘|ISBN=978-4896947847|page=129|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|author=иҸ…еҺҹдҝЎжө·|authorlink=иҸ…еҺҹдҝЎжө·|date=1996-01-01|title=ж—Ҙжң¬жҖқжғігҒЁзҘһд»Ҹзҝ’еҗҲ|publisher=гҖҗжҳҘз§ӢзӨҫгҖ‘|ISBN=978-4393191057|page=24|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|author={{ill|Hamada KЕҚsaku|ja|жөңз”°иҖ•зӯ–|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamada KЕҚsaku|1=KЕҚsaku Hamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11558004}}|date=2005-06|title=4дё–зҙҖгҒ®ж—Ҙйҹ“й–ўдҝӮ|publisher=гҖҗж—Ҙйҹ“жӯҙеҸІе…ұеҗҢз ”з©¶гҖ‘|newspaper=ж—Ҙйҹ“жӯҙеҸІе…ұеҗҢз ”з©¶е ұе‘ҠжӣёпјҲз¬¬1жңҹпјү|url=http://www.jkcf.or.jp/history_arch/first/1/1-01-hamada_j.pdf|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20151018092951/http://www.jkcf.or.jp/history_arch/first/1/1-01-hamada_j.pdf|format=PDF|archivedate=2015-10-18|page=6|qq=}}</ref>
Furthermore, these individualsвҖ”the Doctors of the Five ClassicsвҖ”do not appear in Korean historical sources such as the {{ill|Samguk sagi|en|Samguk sagi|qid=Q485552|lt=Samguk Sagi|1=Samguk sagi}} and {{ill|Samguk yusa|en|Samguk yusa|qid=Q485538|lt=Samguk Yusa|1=Samguk yusa}},<ref>{{Cite news|author={{ill|Matsuki Akichi|ja|жқҫжңЁжҳҺзҹҘ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuki Akichi|1=Akitomo Matsuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q29526079}}|date=1983-10|title=ж¬ҪжҳҺжңқгҒ«жқҘж—ҘгҒ—гҒҹзҷҫжёҲгҒ®еҢ»её«зҺӢжңүзЁңйҷҖгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰ|publisher=гҖҗж—Ҙжң¬еҢ»еҸІеӯҰдјҡгҖ‘|newspaper=ж—Ҙжң¬еҢ»еҸІеӯҰйӣ‘иӘҢ 29(4)|page=450|qq=}}</ref>
Moreover, the Doctors of the Five Classics dispatched by [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] to the {{ill|Wakoku|en|Wakoku|qid=Q10888424|lt=Wa Kingdom|1=Wakoku}}вҖ”namely, {{ill|Duan Yang'er|ja|ж®өжҘҠзҲҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Duan Yang'er|1=Duan Yang'er|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q19939662}}, {{ill|Han Gao'anmo|ja|жјўй«ҳе®үиҢӮ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Han Gao'anmo|1=Han Gao'anmo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q12584316}}, {{ill|Ma Ding'an|ja|йҰ¬дёҒе®ү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ma Ding'an|1=Ma Ding'an|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119380477}}, {{ill|Wang Daoliang|ja|зҺӢйҒ“иүҜ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wang Daoliang|1=Wang Daoliang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119380470}}, {{ill|Wang Liugui|ja|зҺӢжҹіиІҙ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wang Liugui|1=Wang Liugui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119380471}}, {{ill|Wang Baosun|ja|зҺӢдҝқеӯ«|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wang Baosun|1=Wang Baosun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119380473}}, {{ill|Wang Youluota|ja|зҺӢжңүгҘ„йҷҖ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wang Youluota|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зҺӢжңүгғӘгғ§гӮҰйҷҖ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зҺӢжңүгғӘгғ§гӮҰйҷҖ|1=Wang Youluota|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119380475}}, {{ill|Pan Liangfeng|ja|жҪҳйҮҸиұҠ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pan Liangfeng|1=Pan Liangfeng|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119380518}}, and {{ill|Ding Youtuo|ja|дёҒжңүйҷҖ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ding Youtuo|1=Ding Youtuo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119380519}}вҖ”were all Chinese who had become naturalized in Baekje.<ref>{{Cite news|author=м „лҚ•мһ¬|date=2017-07|title=н•ңкөӯ кі лҢҖмӮ¬нҡҢ еӨ–дҫҶдәәмқҳ мЎҙмһ¬м–‘нғңмҷҖ мӮ¬нҡҢм Ғ м—ӯн• |publisher=гҖҗжӘҖеңӢеӨ§еӯёж Ў жқұжҙӢеӯёзЎҸз©¶йҷўгҖ‘|newspaper=жқұжҙӢеӯё з¬¬68ијҜ|url=https://cms.dankook.ac.kr/web/-oriental/-23?p_p_id=Bbs_WAR_bbsportlet&p_p_lifecycle=2&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&p_p_cacheability=cacheLevelPage&p_p_col_id=column-2&p_p_col_count=1&_Bbs_WAR_bbsportlet_extFileId=99960|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20220423195439/https://cms.dankook.ac.kr/web/-oriental/-23?p_p_id=Bbs_WAR_bbsportlet&p_p_lifecycle=2&p_p_state=normal&p_p_mode=view&p_p_cacheability=cacheLevelPage&p_p_col_id=column-2&p_p_col_count=1&_Bbs_WAR_bbsportlet_extFileId=99960|format=PDF|archivedate=2022-04-23|page=110|qq=}}</ref>
Regarding the dispatch of the Doctors of the Five Classics, Japanese scholars generally summarize the opinions into three points. First, that Baekje sent officials ({{ill|officials|ja|е®ҳдәә|1=kanjin|2=ja|3=е®ҳдәә|4=ko|5=кҙҖмқё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/officials|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452075}}) and the Doctors of the Five Classics to Japan as compensation for its territorial expansion ({{ill|Mimana|en|Mimana|qid=Q1790223|lt=Imna|1=Mimana}}). Second, that {{ill|Chinese people|en|Chinese people|qid=Q6501380|lt=Chinese|1=Chinese people}} individuals came to Japan to serve on a rotational basis. Third, that the practice of sending hostages ({{ill|Hostage|en|Hostage|qid=Q192620|lt=hostages|1=Hostage}}) from Baekje to Japan had been institutionalized.<ref name="еҙ”зҰҸе§«"/>
Regarding the second point, {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|е№ійҮҺйӮҰйӣ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|1=Kunio Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11483736}} stated that вҖңwith the exception of the Baekje officials who led the Doctors of the Five Classics, all were from the {{ill|Northern and Southern dynasties|en|Northern and Southern dynasties|qid=Q49696|lt=Southerners|1=Northern and Southern dynasties}}.вҖқ<ref>{{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|author={{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|е№ійҮҺйӮҰйӣ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|1=Kunio Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11483736}}|date=1969-05-01|title=еӨ§еҢ–еүҚд»ЈзӨҫдјҡзө„з№”гҒ®з ”з©¶|series=гҖҗж—Ҙжң¬еҸІеӯҰз ”з©¶еҸўжӣёгҖ‘|isbn=4642020381|publisher=гҖҗеҗүе·қејҳж–ҮйӨЁгҖ‘|page=250-258|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|author={{ill|Choi Bokhee|ja|еҙ”зҰҸе§«|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choi Bokhee|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|date=2002-03-01|title=еҸӨд»Јйҹ“ж—Ҙд»Ҹж•ҷж–ҮеҢ–дәӨжөҒгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰ пјҡ дәәзҡ„дәӨжөҒгҒ«иҰӢгӮүгӮҢгӮӢдәҢгғ»дёүгҒ®е•ҸйЎҢзӮ№гӮ’дёӯеҝғгҒ«|publisher=гҖҗдҪӣж•ҷеӨ§еӯҰгҖ‘|newspaper=дҪӣж•ҷеӨ§еӯёеӨ§еӯёйҷўзҙҖиҰҒ 30|page=30|qq=}}</ref>
